paper_title: Gene Flow Monitoring in P. domestica Orchards

methods.description: Process for monitoring gene flow in P. domestica trees over 11 years, involving sampling from various plots to assess gene flow from genetically engineered (GE) plum trees.

steps.description: Sampled trees were located in nine plots in the orchard including those within the GE plum source block (T0).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and locate trees in orchard plots for sampling

steps.description: Individual trees were, on average, sampled in two of the 11 years which resulted in 115 samples during the course of the study.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples from identified trees over study period

steps.description: Genotypes were selected for sampling based on traits including compatibility with 'HoneySweet' GE plum, time of bloom, and adaptation to the environment.
steps.searchable_summary: Select tree genotypes for sampling based on specific traits

steps.description: Sentinel plots were planted in 2003 with two trees each of the cultivars 'Seneca' and 'Italian Prune'.
steps.searchable_summary: Establish sentinel plots with specific cultivars

steps.description: Monitoring of the sentinel plantings began in 2005 when they were fully flowering.
steps.searchable_summary: Begin monitoring sentinel plots when flowering
